blocks.content: The park at night smelled like grass and popcorn and the particular warmth that came off a Georgia summer after sundown — not cool exactly, but gentler. The movie played. Gene Kelly danced through rain that was pretend rain, and the audience laughed at things that were still funny fifty years later.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I don't know. He's in eighth grade. He plays baseball.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I didn't know about him either until last week.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Do you think she actually likes him?
The screen flickered. On it, someone was in love and trying not to say so, which is the natural condition of most movies and — Kit was starting to understand — of most people.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Is it weird that she didn't say anything?

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: You don't always have to say things out loud.
He looked at her. She was watching the movie. He looked at the movie.
